items.title: Amazonia's Indigenous peoples dismantle Western cliches
items.description: Indigenous peoples and the Amazon rainsforest have often been exoticized. A new exhibition challenges these long-held tropes.

items.title: Ready-to-wear autumn-winter 2026/27 collections: Is fashion still subversive?
items.description: Is fashion still subversive? The answer is yes, with the extraordinarily named Matières Fécales label. At Yohji Yamamoto, it's yes, but more delicately. Satoshi Kondo for Issey Miyake, meanwhile, advocates for movement without constraints. Historian-performer Olivier Saillard elevates everyday clothing with his Living Fashion Museum. Lilia Litkovska, from Ukraine, draws inspiration from her war-torn country. And Ludovic Winterstan, for La Fabrique Nomade, believes it's high time we accepted each other, wherever our country of origin.

items.title: Stocks fall, bonds yields climb as Iran war prompts central bank recalibration
Iran-Israel war: A day‑by‑day rundown of the escalating crisis
items.description: A joint U.S.-Israel attack on Iran and the killing of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei have plunged West Asia into a fast‑spreading war, with Tehran and its allies launching retaliatory strikes across Israel, the Gulf and U.S. bases.

items.title: Bondi Beach attack | From bloodshed to blooms: inside the making of a living memorial
items.description: Installations made with sunflowers, roses, orchids, wattles and bougainvillea left at the 2025 Bondi Beach massacre site will be unveiled at the Sydney Jewish Museum next year

items.title: The power of giving: The role of women donors
items.description: In this edition, we focus on the world of philanthropy, with a spotlight on women donors. This as charities come under growing pressure, amid a shifting political climate that's impacting support for diversity and inclusion and especially as a significant chunk of funding goes to women and girls around the world. MacKenzie Scott, the ex-wife of Amazon founder Jeff Bezos, gave more away in 2025 than either Bezos or fellow billionaire Elon Musk gave away in their entire lifetimes.

items.title: Norwegian princess says she was 'manipulated' by Epstein
items.description: Norway's Crown Princess Mette-Marit gave an interview on her ties to the late US sex offender after new details came to light in the "Epstein files."

items.title: Exiled Iranian writer Shahrnush Parsipur on her International Booker Prize-nominated novella
items.description: Published in 1989, the book was seen as an act of insurrection and Parsipur was jailed, while the novella was banned

items.title: Work from home, avoid air travel to deal with higher energy prices: International Energy Agency
items.description: The U.S-Israel war ‌with Iran has driven up energy prices, causing concerns over inflation ‌across the world

items.title: Global oil and energy prices are reeling as the war in the Middle East escalates
items.description: Israel and Iran continue to exchange airstrikes with a focus on energy infrastructure. President Trump says Israel acted alone in striking a key gas field and better not do it again.

items.title: Tesla expects Dutch decision on self-driving technology by April 10
Leading anti‑racism activist in Tunisia jailed for eight years
items.description: Saadia Mosbah campaigned for migrants, particularly after President Kais Saied said they posed a demographic threat to the country.

items.title: Bayern Munich's Karl and Urbig in latest Germany squad
items.description: Julian Nagelsmann's latest Germany squad includes a host of new names. The 2026 World Cup is less than three months away.

items.title: Sudanese artists rebuild cultural life in wartime shelter
items.description: In a school-turned-shelter on the Red Sea coast, music rises above the hardships of war as displaced Sudanese artists recreate a sense of community and purpose.

items.title: Political tug of war over Iranian women's football team prompts criticism in Australia
items.description: Iran has claimed victory in the extraordinary public relations battle that played out since Australian Immigration Minister Tony Burke released to the media on March 10 a photo of him posing with five women who had accepted protection visas

items.title: Paraguay, underdog Trump ally in Latin America, becomes an investor magnet
items.description: Entrepreneurs flock to Paraguay from across the region as sleek towers rise above the once-sleepy capital and its conservative president cozies up to the US. Leer más

items.title: 'Not our war': Palestinians caught in the crossfire of Middle East war
items.description: Four women were killed in the occupied #WestBank following missile fire from #Iran - the first Palestinian victims of the Middle East #war since it started on February 28. For Palestinians, it is yet another conflict they find themselves caught up in, with little protection.

items.title: Denmark planned to blow up Greenland runways if US invaded, reports say
items.description: Denmark's public broadcaster reports that the US operation to seize Venezuela's leader sparked fears it would act against Greenland.

items.title: Three men charged with conspiring to smuggle U.S. artificial intelligence to China
items.description: The men violated U.S. export controls laws by scheming to divert massive quantities of the high-performance servers assembled in the United States to China between 2024 and 2025

items.title: Hungary's Orbán accused of disloyalty and blackmail over Ukraine loan veto
items.description: The Hungarian leader says a pipeline carrying Russian oil through Ukraine to Hungary must be repaired before any funds can be released.

items.title: South Korea: Factory fire injures dozens, workers trapped
items.description: A fire at a Daejeon auto factory prompted President Lee Jae Myung to order a full rescue operation. Workers were seen jumping from windows to escape the flames.

items.title: Tesla in talks with Chinese firms to buy $2.9 bln worth of solar equipment
items.description: Mr. Musk plans to build the solar capacity mainly for use by Tesla, although some will be used to power SpaceX satellites

items.title: Tunisian anti-racism activist sentenced to eight years in prison
items.description: A Tunisian court on Thursday sentenced anti-racism activist Saadia Mosbah to eight years in prison on financial misconduct charges, a ruling her lawyer condemned as "shocking" and politically motivated, marking the latest crackdown on civil society in the North African country.

items.title: Moroccans turn to gold as safe haven investment
items.description: In Morocco, the gold business is booming. The precious metal is seen as a safe investment to hold wealth.

items.title: Pinterest CEO calls for ban on social media for youth under 16
Several injured as turbulence hits Delta flight into Sydney
items.description: Three flight attendants were taken to hospital after the Airbus A350 experienced turbulence during its descent into Sydney Airport.

items.title: Finland is world's happiest country ninth year in a row, UN happiness report finds
items.description: Finland maintains its top position as the world's happiest country for the ninth year running, as it scores 7.764 on a 10-point scale, according to an annual UN-sponsored report. Known for its nature, sauna culture and extensive welfare system, the country's 5.6 million inhabitants generally report a high level of trust in authorities and low levels of inequality.

items.title: Iran women’s soccer team greeted with a welcome ceremony in Tehran
items.description: Members of Iran’s national women’s soccer team were greeted with a welcome ceremony upon their return to the Islamic Republic after several of the players sought asylum in Australia

items.title: Trump makes Pearl Harbor remark in meeting with Japan's PM
items.description: President Trump compared US strikes on Iran to the deadly 1941 Japanese attack and declaration of war as part of World War Two.

items.title: Israel hits Tehran with airstrikes on Persian New Year as war jolts energy markets
items.description: Heavy explosions shook Dubai as air defenses intercepted incoming fire over the city, where people were observing Id-Ul-Fitr, the end of the holy Muslim fasting month of Ramzan

items.title: Mexican teen dies while being held in US immigration custody
items.description: Royer Perez-Jimenez died amid a surge of migrant deaths under Donald Trump's immigration crackdown.

items.title: ByteDance to sell gaming unit Moonton to Saudi PIF-owned firm
Alice Springs prison guards go on 12-hour strike over 'unsafe' conditions
items.description: Alice Springs prison guards have taken industrial action, including a 12-hour work stoppage across the town's correctional facilities, over what they say are unsafe conditions.

items.title: Zelenskyy says Ukraine wants timeline for next round of Russia talks
items.description: Volodymyr Zelenskyy says 'clear dates' needed as Ukrainian negotiators prepare for discussions in US.

French navy seizes oil tanker sailing from Russia accused of flying false flag in Mediterranean
items.description: The French navy has boarded and seized an oil tanker sailing in the Western ‌Mediterranean from the Russian port of Murmansk accused of belonging to what has been dubbed Moscow's "shadow fleet", vessels of opaque ownership suspected of dodging Western sanctions on the country's crude oil exports.

items.title: Trump draws parallel between Pearl Harbor and U.S. strikes on Iran in meeting with Japanese PM
items.description: The Japanese attack on ‌the U.S. naval base in Pearl Harbor, Hawaii on Dec. 7, 1941, killed 2,390 Americans, and the U.S. declared war on Japan the next day

items.title: Eid al-Fitr celebrations begin under shadow of war
items.description: Eid al-Fitr, which marks the end of the holy Muslim month of Ramadan, began this Friday in countries across the globe. But in many parts of the world, the celebrations are being overshadowed by war.

items.title: Dana Lixenberg's personal, political and pared-down portraits
items.description: Her photographs of celebrated personalities, from Toni Morrison to Tupac Shakur, have become iconic images. Dana Lixenberg tells us about three decades of providing her unique perspective on the United States and the evolution of a society she first started documenting in the 1990s. As her major, monographic exhibition "American Images" opens at the Maison Européenne de la Photo in Paris, the Dutch photographer tells us about being surprised by the enduring success of her beautiful black-and-white image of Tupac, how a small community in South Central LA crystallised her love of image-making and why artifice in photography is all right in very small doses.

items.title: Iran’s IRGC says spokesman Ali Mohammad Naini killed in US-Israeli attack
items.description: Israeli and US air attacks pound Iran as assassination campaign of country's leadership continues.

items.title: Who are the Gulf’s military allies, and how are they helping in Iran war?
items.description: Experts say while Gulf states need support in face of Iranian attacks, it is less clear what their allies can provide.

items.title: Muslims celebrate Eid al-Fitr around the world amid shadow of war
items.description: People gather with family and for congregational prayers to mark the end of the holy fasting month.

items.title: Sahel accounts for nearly half of all terror-related deaths: report
items.description: The Sahel region of Africa, which has become the "global epicentre of terrorism," accounted for nearly half of all terrorism-related deaths for the third consecutive year in 2025, the Global Terrorism Report said Thursday.

items.title: Ros Atkins on... Trump's mixed messages on the war
items.description: The BBC’s Analysis Editor looks at President Trump’s mixed messages on the war with Iran.

items.title: Katherine braces for second major flood in two weeks as cyclone approaches NT
items.description: Just two weeks after its worst flood in almost 30 years, Katherine businesses are being sandbagged and a field hospital has been set up as locals brace for another major weather event.

items.title: Brisbane pull off grand final repeat in rematch with Storm
items.description: The stakes might not have been as high as last year's grand final but Brisbane followed the same script with a rollicking comeback win over Melbourne in the rematch of last year's decider.

items.title: What is France hoping to achieve with FM's Middle East visit?
items.description: French Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot visited Israel Friday after a trip to Lebanon's Beirut the day before. It is part of a push by France for Israel to hold direct talks with the Lebanese government in what would be an historic first. But what is France hoping to achieve with the talks? France 24's James Andre takes a look.

items.title: Joint statement on Strait of Hormuz by European nations, Japan, Canada
items.description: This is ⁠the full text of the statement from Britain, France, Germany, Italy, the Netherlands, Japan and Canada

items.title: Why it's so hard for world leaders to bring down oil and gasoline prices
items.description: From waiving the Jones Act to rerouting oil through the Red Sea, governments are doing their best to make up for the crisis in the Strait of Hormuz, but prices are still rising.

items.title: 'Transitional moment': Leaders make final pitches ahead of SA election, One Nation leading Liberals in latest poll
items.description: The major parties have made their final campaign pitches to South Australian voters as a Newspoll survey puts primary support for One Nation at 22 per cent, ahead of the Liberals on just 16 per cent. One political analyst says it is a "transitional moment in South Australian politics".

items.title: Nasa's Artemis Moon rocket rolls back to pad for possible April launch
items.description: Nasa is preparing a mission to send astronauts around the Moon for the first time in more than 50 years.

items.description: The U.S. Justice Department's release of ⁠millions of Epstein documents has sent shockwaves around the world, revealing the disgraced financier's ties to prominent people, including the crown ‌princess and top Norwegian politicians, business executives and diplomats

items.title: Affordability mandate dropped from Qld housing supply scheme
items.description: The LNP government says the move is about cutting red tape and delivering homes more quickly but, Labor claims it will further reduce affordability.

items.title: Gulf crisis draws attention to African oil [Business Africa]
items.description: Unlike crude from the middle east, a lot of African oil does not have to transit vulnerable corridors such as the Strait of Hormuz, where events like war can put a stop to shipping. But years of constrained investment meant African producers cannot pump more and fast enough to cover supply gaps

items.title: Eid under siege: Little to celebrate in Gaza as Israel tightens chokehold
items.description: Tighter restrictions on goods and aid are driving shortages, adding to the strain on families in Gaza on Eid.

items.title: Two men charged after violent assault of woman outside Darwin nightclub
items.description: Two men have been charged over an attack outside a Darwin nightclub where a female security worker was allegedly struck with a glass and kicked in the head while unconscious.

items.title: U.S. NTSB launches safety probe after Alaska Airlines jet overflies FedEx at Newark
items.description: “An air traffic controller instructed ‌Alaska Airlines Flight 294, a Boeing 737, to perform a go-around because FedEx Flight 721, ‌a Boeing 777, was cleared for the ‌final ⁠approach to an intersecting runway at the ⁠New Jersey airport near New York City,” says FAA

items.title: Epworth releases details of hospital review after surgeon allegations
items.description: A South Australian health commissioner will lead the review of clinical governance at Epworth Hospital, after allegations a top gynaecologist performed unnecessary operations to remove endometriosis.

items.title: Bulldog Baker's brilliant goal seals thrilling win over fast-finishing Crows
items.description: The Western Bulldogs hang on to claim a thrilling six-point win over Adelaide, with a brilliant Oskar Baker goal clinching the win as the Crows stormed home.

items.title: French tycoon Vincent Bolloré to stand trial in ‘African ports’ corruption case
items.description: French industrialist Vincent Bolloré will stand trial in December before the Paris criminal court in the so‑called “African ports” case.

items.title: Former childcare worker jailed for indecent treatment of child
items.description: A former childcare worker from Fiji is sentenced to jail for the indecent treatment of a child at a daycare centre in Queensland, and will be deported afterwards.

items.title: Teenagers sue Musk’s xAI claiming image-generator made sexually explicit images of them as minors
items.description: Other AI companies have prohibited their image-generators from producing any sexually explicit content, even of adults. Mr. Musk saw this as a business opportunity and promoted the ability of xAI's Grok chatbot to create “spicy” content, the lawsuit claims.

items.title: Four in ten workers in Argentina are informally employed, UBA report finds
items.description: UBA report says that seven in ten workers aged between 16 and 24 are in informal employment, reflecting the difficulties facing this labour group. Leer más

items.title: Tributes pour in from across the world for South African peace advocate
items.description: Anti-apartheid activist, human rights campaigner, and constitutional lawyer, Nicholas “Fink” Haysom, died on Wednesday aged 73.

items.title: North Korea’s Kim Jong Un takes his daughter on tank ride during army training
items.description: State media images showed the girl sticking her head out of the tank’s hatch, while Kim sitting on the top of the tank and smiling

items.title: Long before Trump: How US policy has harmed the environment for decades
items.description: Environment advocacy groups are suing the US government over climate change policy rollback.

items.title: Despite decapitation, Iran’s regime endures: Does Hormuz blockade give Tehran the upper hand?
items.description: William Hilderbrandt is pleased to welcome Paul Taylor, Senior Visiting Fellow in the Europe in the World Programme and a member of the Defence/Security EUrope project. He is also a freelance columnist for The Guardian. Amid the Strait of Hormuz blockade, Mr. Taylor argues that even overwhelming force cannot guarantee safety when asymmetric tools, such as drones and decentralised command structures, remain effective. At its core, he contends that reopening the Strait is not a technical or logistical challenge, but a political and diplomatic one.

items.title: BTS return from military service
items.description: The band's 10th album sees them return to the rebellious, rap-heavy sound of their early days.
items.source: but does their album live up to the hype?

items.title: Man found guilty of killing passenger during drug-fuelled road trip
items.description: A Supreme Court jury delivers a verdict in the case of two men charged over a string of public shootings on the NSW mid north coast in 2023.

items.title: Germany: Police dismantle major cyberattack 'botnets'
items.description: German authorities have shut down two of the world’s largest "botnets" as part of a joint three-nation operation. Each of the networks hijacked millions of compromised everyday devices to take part in cyberattacks.

items.title: Switzerland announces halt to weapons exports to US amid Iran war
items.description: Move comes as Swiss government also closed its airspace to US military flights directly linked to the Iran war.

items.title: Iran today, Africa tomorrow
items.description: What is being done to Iran today could be done to Africa tomorrow. Africa must reject this illegal war.

items.title: India news: Indian national killed in Riyadh strike
items.description: India's embassy in Riyadh said an Indian national was killed on March 18, bringing the total number of Indian victims in the Middle East to six.

items.description: The World Trade Organization warned Thursday that prolonged high oil and gas prices in 2026 would "shave off" growth from an already grim trade forecast.

items.title: Rwanda, DRC agree to "ease tensions" after talks in Washington
items.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo and Rwanda agreed during talks in Washington to take "concrete steps" to ease tensions and advance a peace accord struck last year, a joint statement said.

items.title: Nvidia to sell 1 million chips to Amazon by end of 2027 in cloud deal
'Asking why honey is better than s***': Japan coach spins favouritism ahead of Matildas clash
items.description: Japan's coach says determining a favourite for the Women's Asian Cup final between his team and Australia is a matter for bees and flies.

items.description: Israeli air strikes target army camps in response to alleged attacks on the Druze community in Suwayda on Thursday.

items.description: The Pentagon is seeking $200 billion in additional funds for the Iran war, a senior administration official says

items.title: Paramedic care 'below reasonable standard' in death in custody, coroner finds
items.description: A coroner has found paramedics failed to meet standards of care for a man who died from cardiac arrhythmia while being restrained by police.

items.title: Indonesian billionaire Michael Bambang Hartono dies at 86
items.description: The cause of death was not immediately clear. Alongside his brother Robert, Budi Hartono, Michael Hartono was worth an estimated $43.8 billion, according to Forbes in 2025, putting them at the top of Indonesia's rich list

items.description: The price of Russian Urals has soared in recent weeks. How Russia - and other energy producing nations - could profit.

items.description: This lawsuit comes less than two ‌months after President Trump said his administration was seeking $1 billion from Harvard to settle probes into school policies

items.title: EU leaders vent anger after Hungary's Orban keeps blocking Ukraine loan
items.description: European Union leaders on Thursday lashed out at Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, accusing him of hijacking critical aid for Ukraine and undermining EU decision-making in an effort to win an election at home. France 24's correspondent Dave Keating has the latest from Brussels.

items.title: A look at the economic cost of the war in the Middle East
items.description: The team from NPR's The Indicator podcast takes a look at the economic costs of the war in the Middle East.

items.title: Trump's mediators offer Hamas a formal proposal to give up its weapons in Gaza
items.description: President Trump's Board of Peace has quietly given Hamas a proposal to hand over all of its weapons to ensure Gaza's reconstruction.

items.title: BTS: Everything you need to know about the K-pop comeback of the year
items.description: After a four-year hiatus and the end of their military service, BTS are returning as a full group. With a new album, a Netflix livestream and a major world tour on the horizon, the K-pop icons are entering a new chapter.

items.description: Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesman Lin Jian told a media briefing that envoys are an important bridge for fostering friendly and cooperative relations between nations

items.description: Actor, martial artist and the source of a meme about him being unbeatable, has passed away.

items.title: France presenting 'nuanced position' as FM Barrot visits Israel
items.description: French Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot said Friday on a visit to Israel that Iran must make major concessions as part of any long-term political solution following the Middle East war, while also calling on Israel to seize an "historic opportunity" and hold direct talks with Lebanese authorities. France 24’s Noga Tarnopolsky said Barrot’s comments show France is trying to take a more “nuanced” position as it seeks to help mediate an end to the conflict.

items.title: King stakes claim for Aussie World Cup recall after controversial omission
items.description: Alana King gives Australia's selectors a timely reminder of the quality she possesses, helping secure a win against the West Indies.

items.title: Watch: Gabbard faces scrutiny over Iran nuclear intelligence claims
items.description: The US Director of National Intelligence, Tulsi Gabbard, was questioned over what Democrats perceive as a discrepancy between White House and intelligence community claims.

items.title: Rio's favela guides learn French to meet surging tourist demand
items.description: Brazil’s Rio welcomed over 2 million tourists last year and among them, an increasing number of French visitors, many of whom are being drawn to the city’s favelas. Visits to the favelas have become trendy on social media, prompting a new generation of guides to learn French to meet the growing demand.

items.title: Niger rejects EU's call to free ousted President Bazoum
items.description: The junta government has rebuffed a European Parliament resolution demanding the release of ex-President Bazoum. It further warned the EU to stop meddling in Niger's internal affairs.

items.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 21 of US-Israel attacks?
items.description: Tehran has warned of zero restraint if energy facilities are attacked again, while Netanyahu points to ground operation.

items.title: Burkina Faso’s tomato export ban raises concerns over regional supply
items.description: Burkina Faso’s decision to ban the export of fresh tomatoes is drawing attention across West Africa, with traders and consumers watching closely for possible effects on supply and prices.

items.title: Trump is dismantling democracy, reports find. And, Treasury to take over student loans
items.description: Recent studies show the U.S. is slipping further from democracy. And, the Trump administration plans to transfer federal student loans from the Education Department to the Treasury Department.

items.title: Nowruz in London: Iranian diaspora marks spring amid grief over war in Iran
items.description: Members of the Iranian diaspora in north London gathered on 20 March to mark Nowruz, the Persian New Year, despite concern over the ongoing war in Iran.

items.title: A cultural stroll through Paris with singer Yael Naim and comedian Philippine Delaire
items.description: This week Valérie Fayolle sits down with Yael Naim, who takes us round the Gaîté Lyrique, at the beating heart of the Paris cultural scene. She tells us about her seventh album, "Solaire", an ode to freedom, imperfection and femininity. Next up is vivacious Philippine Delaire, treading the boards of the Comédie de Paris. The actress and comedian is the star of "Daddy's Girl".

items.title: I didn't know Epstein was a predator
items.description: Norway's Crown Princess Mette-Marit has said that she wishes she had never met late US sex offender Jeffrey Epstein

items.title: Salman Rushdie on why tyrants fear artists

items.title: One Nation candidate wanted in UK on sexual touching charge
items.description: South Australian One Nation candidate Aoi Baxter has a warrant for his arrest in the UK after he failed to show up to court to face a charge of sexually touching a woman without consent.

items.title: Japan's Takaichi tries to reaffirm alliance with Trump as he seeks help securing Strait of Hormuz
items.description: Ms. Takaichi, who met with Mr. Trump at the White House, told the Republican President that Japan has opposed Iran's development of its nuclear programme and appealed to his desire to be seen as a peacemaker, despite his launching a war of choice with Iran

items.title: K-Pop stars BTS release new album ahead of comeback concert
items.description: The BTS comeback concert, to be held at Seoul's historic Gwanghwamun Square, is expected to draw around 260,000 people. The event will be livestreamed by Netflix in around 190 countries.

items.title: Iran does not need to close the Strait of Hormuz to disrupt it
items.description: The real risk lies not in closure, but in disruption in the strait’s approaches.

items.description: Women's Asian Cup hosts Australia face criticism for the political tug of war over the Iranian women's football team.

items.description: Young people have a “much lower” level of wellbeing than 15 years ago due to the effects of social media use, according to the 2026 edition of the World Happiness Report, which sees Finland maintain its position as the world’s happiest country for the ninth consecutive year.

items.description: The BBC's Will Grant reports from the island days after the entire national electric grid collapsed.

items.title: Madagascar’s president orders polygraph for aspiring ministers
items.description: Madagascar's military leader, President Michael Randrianirina, announced Thursday that anyone applying to become a minister in the new government will undergo lie detector tests to screen for corruption—a rare and unprecedented vetting measure.

items.description: Red Crescent teams rescue survivors in South Khorasan after reported US-Israeli strikes, as Iran counts rising civilian toll and mounting destruction.

items.description: Far North Queensland residents say Tropical Cyclone Narelle has left a trail of damage across Cape York.

items.title: EU eyes energy tax cuts to counter impact of Iran war
items.description: The war in the Middle East is having an increasingly significant impact on energy markets. In recent days, strikes by both Iran and Israel have caused extensive damage to the region’s energy infrastructure, and prices are skyrocketing. Meeting in Brussels for a European summit, European leaders discussed measures to be implemented to curb the surge in prices, including lifting caps on state aid for energy-intensive industrial sectors and reducing taxes on electricity.

items.title: Colombia: Baby grey titi monkey gets cuddly ‘mum’ ahead of return to wild
items.description: An orphaned grey titi monkey was released back into the wild after rehabilitation at a wildlife centre in Antioquia, authorities said on 18 March.

items.title: Iran war: Trump lashes out at NATO over lack of Hormuz help

items.title: Europe's housing crisis: Is the EU plan up to the job?
items.description: MEPs recently approved a key report on housing, with the stated aim of securing decent, sustainable and affordable housing. They are responding to an EU-wide crisis in which property prices jumped by 15.5 percent between 2021 and 2024, according to Eurostat. That has frozen out millions of people looking to get onto the property ladder. Rental prices have continued to go up as well. Housing is a competence of member states, but there has been a push to do more at the EU level. We debate just how much Brussels should get involved, and whether the solutions it is proposing are workable.

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: No clandestine activities in Canada linked to Indian government: Police chief
Giddey sets new Aussie NBA record as Bulls fall short of miracle comeback
items.description: Josh Giddey continues his remarkable form but it is not enough to give the Chicago Bulls a much-needed win.

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: Backlash against sex ed threatens Poland's health curriculum
items.description: The government's decision to introduce voluntary health education at schools caused uproar last year. Supporters say the subject prepares young people for life, but conservatives fear that it will lead children astray.

items.title: ‘It helps us survive’: Poverty forces children into mine work in DR Congo
items.description: Congolese authorities say 70 children died in a landslide at the Rubaya mine, source of much of the world’s coltan.

items.title: Norway's Crown Princess says she was 'deceived' by Epstein
items.description: Norway's Crown Princess has told national TV that she had been "manipulated and deceived" by Jeffrey Epstein and wished she had never met him.

items.title: Hong Kong listings rush gathers pace with five companies seeking $678 million

items.description: President Milei cannot be faulted for his misfortune in timing Argentina Week in New York for what was Iran Week for the rest of the world and he obviously occupied centre stage, but sometimes what happens off-Broadway can be more decisive. Leer más

items.title: Trump compares attack on Iran to Pearl Harbor in meeting with Japanese PM
items.description: In a meeting with Japanese Prime Minister, US President Donald Trump was asked why he didn't inform allies about his plan to attack Iran.

items.title: Crowds flock to Antananarivo as Madagascar restores Its pre‑colonial new year
items.description: Madagascar's traditional New Year, Alahamady Be, a pre-colonial custom abandoned for decades, has now been revived with rituals honouring ancestors, sacred fire and purification ceremonies.

items.title: Historian talks about how Trump is forging a new world order
items.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with historian Daniel Immerwahr about how President Trump is forging a new world order through his foreign policy.

items.title: Trump vows to 'take' Cuba as island reels from oil embargo
items.description: "I do believe I'll be...having the honor of taking Cuba," Trump tells reporters at the White House. Leer más

items.title: Argentina finalises withdrawal from World Health Organization after one-year process
items.description: Milei's government confirms departure after one-year notice period; Foreign Minister Quirno insists country will remain engaged in global health efforts through regional and bilateral channels. Leer más

items.title: Congolese citizens welcome Belgian court trial in Lumumba case
items.description: Several Congolese citizens welcomed a Belgian court's decision to put 93-year-old former diplomat Etienne Davignon on trial over the killing of independence hero Patrice Lumumba in 1961. Africanews correspondent Chris Ocamringa reports from Kinshasa.

items.title: Middle East war boosts traffic through Kenyan port of Lamu
items.description: Thousands of high-end cars being shipped from Japan and destined for Dubai have been dropped off on the Kenyan island of Lamu, port authorities said, as the Mideast war provides a boost to African shipping hubs.

items.title: Major rally planned as Czech MPs mull 'foreign agent' law
items.description: Prague could see one of its largest demonstrations in recent years on Saturday as many prepare to protest what organizers of the rally describe as democratic backsliding under Prime Minister Andrej Babis.

items.title: Court in Buenos Aires orders blocking of Polymarket in Argentina
items.description: Ruling says prediction platform operates as unlicensed online betting system and lacks identity checks; Controversy over US$91,000 in bets on inflation figure placed ahead of official data release. Leer más

items.title: Boca Juniors unveils 80,000-capacity La Bombonera expansion plan
items.description: Riquelme says he has an initial permit from Ferrosur, the company responsible for the railway line that runs just metres from the Xeneize’s stadium, to embark upon the project. Leer más

items.title: South Africa's traditional circumcision claims dozens of young lives
items.description: At least 48 boys and young men died during recent initiation ceremonies in South Africa, among them 22-year-old Lamkelo Mtyho, who had no known health issues when he entered a registered school—but never returned.

items.title: Catalan musician Joan Manuel Serrat says during Argentina trip that individualism leads ‘to the abyss’
items.description: Catalan singer-songwriter Joan Manuel Serrat warns in a speech while receiving honorary degree in Mendoza that individualism leads "to the abyss." Leer más

items.title: Fire at Kuwait refinery as Gulf states come under drone, missile attacks
items.description: Iran has stepped up attacks on Gulf energy infrastructure in recent days, hitting refineries and the world’s biggest gas hub in Qatar, as Tehran retaliated over Israeli strikes on its South Pars gas field

items.description: News of the hosting bid comes ahead of a visit by Alan Gilpin, the chief executive of the World Rugby international federation, to Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Probe revived into ‘$LIBRA’ crypto scam creates fresh headache for Milei
items.description: Opposition lawmakers reopen probe after reports of calls, messages and a draft US$5-million promotion deal linked to the LIBRA cryptocurrency. Leer más

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: Indian killed in Riyadh due to ‘recent events of March 18’, says Embassy
items.description: The Indian embassy said that it is in contact with the deceased’s family and local authorities but the circumstances regarding the Indian national’s death remain unclear

items.title: Drone defense in the Iran war: What can Ukraine offer?
items.description: As the Iran war continues apace, Ukraine's hard-won expertise in countering drone swarms is sparking interest around the world.

items.title: Daily Quiz: On U.S. war on Iraq
items.description: The U.S. launched the Iraq war on this day in 2003. It lasted until 2011 and killed an estimated 1.5 lakh to 10 lakh people

items.title: Fire at South Korean auto parts factory injures at least 53
items.description: Some people were injured while jumping from the building during escape attempts, while others suffered from smoke inhalation, the fire chief of the city’s Daedeok district said. Police were tracking mobile phone signals of the 14 people still unaccounted for.

items.title: News24 | King Charles hosts President Tinubu in first state visit by a Nigerian leader in 37 years
items.description: King Charles III praised Britain’s “partnership of equals” with Nigeria as he hosted a banquet for President Bola Tinubu during the first state visit by the leader of Africa’s most populous nation in nearly four decades.

items.title: No more big spenders: Iran war to dent Gulf state investment
items.description: Gulf states like Saudi Arabia, Qatar and the UAE have invested, or promised to invest, trillions around the world, everywhere from America to Africa. The Iran war may see their money spent closer to home.

items.title: Ukraine sends advisers to Gulf as it counterattacks Russian forces in south
items.description: Ukraine may have reclaimed 400sq km (154sq miles) of territory in 2026, and is downing record numbers of Russian drones.

items.title: News24 | Iran missile strike kills 3 Palestinians in West Bank, Thai worker
items.description: Iranian missile attacks have killed three Palestinian women in the occupied West Bank and a foreign worker in central Israel, medics said.

items.title: Perth man who killed partner then set her alight to spend 24 years in jail
items.description: A Perth man who murdered his partner, a mother of four, before setting her on fire has been sentenced to life in prison with a non-parole period of 24 years.

items.title: Iran hits Kuwaiti oil refinery as Israel renews attacks on Tehran
items.description: Attacks stretch from Kuwait to the UAE as analysts warn conflict is entering a dangerous new phase.

items.title: News24 | US Chief Justice warns of dangerous ‘personally directed hostility’ after Trump criticism
items.description: US Chief Justice John Roberts said that hostility directed in personal terms at judges is “dangerous and it’s got to stop”, commenting just days after US President Donald Trump’s latest social media broadside against judges ‌who have ruled against him and his administration.

items.title: Netflix sees more prospects for live events in South Korea after BTS comeback
items.description: The concert at Seoul's historic Gwanghwamun Square on Mar 21 will mark the ​release of the seven-member group's first new album in more than three ​years and the start of a global tour in April.

items.title: 'Online diplomacy': How Trump narrates war in Iran on Truth Social
items.description: PRESS REVIEW – Friday, March 20: Donald Trump is turning to "online diplomacy" to defend his actions in Iran and lambast his critics on Truth Social. The Times of London looks at how Monica Witt, a US servicewoman-turned-spy for Iran, could play a crucial role in the war. Also: a Belgian court charges a former diplomat with involvement in the killing of Patrice Lumumba, the first prime minister of independent DR Congo. Plus: a little possum is found among plush toys at an airport gift shop in Australia.

items.title: Slovenia election: a battle for the soul of the country?
items.description: Sunday's parliamentary election in Slovenia is shaping up to be a tight race between the governing center-left GS and the right-wing SDS. Nevertheless, smaller parties are likely to hold the key to forming a government.

items.title: Bola Tinubu's state visit to UK: major steel deal agreed with British PM
items.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer welcomed Nigerian President Bola Tinubu at 10 Downing Street in London during the first state visit by the leader of Africa's most populous nation in nearly four decades.

items.title: China poses pressing threat, deterrence needed to avert invasion, Taiwan says
items.description: China does not currently plan to invade Taiwan in 2027 and seeks to control the island without the use of ‌force, says the U.S. intelligence community

items.title: Three charged in the US with smuggling AI chips into China
items.description: US charges men reportedly tied to Super Micro Computer for helping to smuggle billions of dollars' worth of AI chips.

items.title: French billionaire Bolloré to face corruption trial linked to African elections
items.description: French ⁠billionaire Vincent Bolloré will stand trial on charges of embezzlement and corruption related to election campaigns in two African countries. Bolloré is ⁠suspected of having bribed foreign officials ‌during the election campaign of presidents Faure Gnassingbe ⁠in Togo and Alpha Conde in Guinea, prosecutors said.

items.title: UN mine action chiefs for Ethiopia and Sudan call for more funding
items.description: They say mines kill and maim a disproportionate number of children and that people cannot return to safety without addressing explosive hazards.

items.title: 'Vulnerable' satellites guide the world — and its wars
items.description: From smartphone maps to precision weapons, satellite navigation underpins modern life and war. Global Navigation Satellite Systems have become indispensable, and that's made them vulnerable to disruption and attack.

items.title: EU asks for reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and no more strikes on energy, water sites
items.description: European leaders have deflected entreaties from U.S. President Donald Trump to send military assets to secure the Strait of Hormuz, a key waterway for the global flow of oil, gas and fertilizer

items.title: Argentina to face Guatemala friend in place of scrapped Finalissima
items.description: World champions will play Guatemala in a friendly – venue set to be confirmed – in place ofe the cancelled Finalissima match against European champions Spain in Qatar. Leer más

items.title: Israel launches more strikes on Tehran as Iran continues attacks on Gulf oil facilities
items.description: The latest strikes come after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Thursday that Israel would "hold off on future attacks" on Iran's energy infrastructure, following Trump's request.

items.title: Cristina Fernández de Kirchner goes on attack as she appears in court for ‘Cuadernos’ trial
items.description: Former president summoned for questioning in connection with ‘Cuadernos’ case; She delivers statement attacking President Milei, Judiciary, government, but refuses to answer questions. Leer más
